title: hieracium nigrescens subsp. haglundii
intro: Hieracium nigrescens subsp. haglundii, also known as Haglund's hawkweed, is a rare and lesser-known species of flowering plant in the dandelion family (Asteraceae). This plant has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating digestive issues, such as diarrhea and stomach cramps, due to its anthelmintic and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Hieracium nigrescens subsp. haglundii include its ability to act as a natural remedy for gastrointestinal problems, as well as its potential to support immune function and reduce inflammation. Herbal preparations of this plant often include infusions and tinctures, although more research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety as a medicinal herb.
